title: Smoky Gooey Uruguayan Sour Cream Casserole
description: A hearty, hangover-curing casserole featuring caramelized onions and a crispy, smoky cheddar crust. Perfect for a lazy Sunday morning.

ingredients.name: Caramelized onions
ingredients.notes: Cook until dark brown and caramelized.

ingredients.name: Bacon
ingredients.notes: Use thick-cut, smoked bacon for the best flavor.

instructions: Brown the bacon in a skillet over medium heat.
Add the flour and cook for 1 minute, stirring constantly.
Slowly add the sour cream, whisking constantly.
Bring the mixture to a simmer and cook until thickened, about 2-3 minutes.
Stir in the caramelized onions and smoked cheddar cheese.
Season with salt and black pepper to taste.
Transfer the mixture to a 9x13 inch baking dish.
Sprinkle the top with additional cheese and chopped chives or parsley if desired.
Bake for 25-30 minutes, or until the top is golden brown and the casserole is hot and bubbly.
Remove from the oven and let rest for 10 minutes before serving.
Cut into squares and serve hot.
Garnish with additional chives or parsley if desired.
Note: This casserole is best served fresh, but it can be refrigerated overnight and reheated in the oven or microwave.
